title: Pseudorabies virus (prv) vector expressing heterologous polypeptides
abstract: This application describes a viral vector comprising a pseudorabies virus with at least two genomic deletions that eliminate glycoprotein E (gE) and thymidine kinase (TK) expression. In some cases, the viral vector can also have a deletion in a glycoprotein G locus. The viral vector can also include one or more heterologous nucleic acid segments. For example, the heterologous nucleic acid segments can encode one or more classical swine fever viral (CSFV) or porcine circovirus 2 (PCV2) polypeptide or peptides.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED: 1. A viral vector comprising a pseudorabies virus with at least two genomic deletions that eliminate glycoprotein E (gE) and thymidine kinase (TK) expression. 2. The viral vector of claim 1, wherein the viral vector comprises a glycoprotein E (gE) gene deletion sequence with at least 95% sequence identity to SEQ ID NO:l or SEQ ID NO:2 when no heterologous nucleic acid segments are inserted into the glycoprotein E (gE) locus. 3. The viral vector of claim 1 , wherein the viral vector comprises a thymidine kinase gene deletion sequence with at least 95% sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 8, SEQ ID NO: 11, or SEQ ID NO: 12 when no heterologous nucleic acid segments are inserted into the thymidine kinase (TK) locus. 4. The viral vector of claim 1, further comprising a genomic deletion that eliminates glycoprotein G (gG) expression. 5. The viral vector of claim 1, wherein the viral vector comprises a glycoprotein G (gG) deletion sequence with at least 95% sequence identity to SEQ ID NO:53 when no heterologous nucleic acid segments are inserted into the glycoprotein G (gG) locus. 6. The viral vector of claim 1, further comprising one or more heterologous nucleic acid segments selected from a nucleic acid segment encoding an E2 polypeptide or peptide, a nucleic acid segment encoding a capsid polypeptide or peptide, a nucleic acid segment encoding a Eâ¢, or a combination thereof inserted into the viral vector. 7. The viral vector of claim 1, further comprising one or more heterologous nucleic acid segments selected from a classical swine fever viral heterologous nucleic acid segment encoding an E2 polypeptide or peptide, a porcine circovirus 2 (PCV2) nucleic acid segment encoding a capsid polypeptide or peptide, a classical swine fever viral heterologous nucleic acid segment encoding an E"*, or a combination thereof inserted into the viral vector. 8. The viral vector of claim 1 , further comprising one or more heterologous nucleic acid segments encoding a protein with a sequence having at least 95% sequence identity to SEQ ID NO:3, 6, 13, 15, 19, 21, 23, 24, 25, 49, or 51. 9. The viral vector of claim 6, wherein the one or more heterologous nucleic acid segments encode a fusion protein comprising a heterologous polypeptide or peptide and a fusion partner of the heterologous polypeptide or peptide. 10. The viral vector of claim 6, wherein the one or more heterologous nucleic acid segments encode a fusion protein comprising an heterologous polypeptide or peptide 

 and a fusion partner selected from a signal peptide, a VS peptide, a TK peptide, a GM-CSF peptide, or combinations thereof. 11. The viral vector of claim 1, wherein the viral vector comprises a glycoprotein G (gG) deletion sequence with at least 95% sequence identity to SEQ ID NO:48. 12. A viral vector comprising a PRVgEA/CSFVE2+ TK null/del- PCV2Cap+ virus. 13. A composition comprising a carrier and a viral vector comprising a pseudorabies virus with at least two genomic deletions that eliminate glycoprotein E (gE) and thymidine kinase (TK) expression. 14. A method comprising administering to a mammal a composition comprising a carrier and a viral vector comprising a pseudorabies virus with at least two genomic deletions that eliminate glycoprotein E (gE) and thymidine kinase (TK) expression. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the mammal is a pig. 16. The method of claim 14, wherein the mammal is a baby pig, a pregnant female pig, an adult female pig, or an adult male pig.
